title: Minisforum’s Upcoming MS-03 SFF PC Brings More of a Good Thing
gist: Minisforum's upcoming MS-03 mini-PC keeps the popular MS-01 chassis unchanged but upgrades to Intel's Core Ultra Series 3 (Panther Lake) H-series SoC, adding an NPU, DDR5-7200, Wi-Fi 7, and PCIe Gen5 storage, while the author flags its 2.5GbE RJ45 jacks and limited front USB as dated for 2026.

insights.claim: Minisforum's MS-03 mini-PC is built around Intel's Core Ultra Series 3 (Panther Lake) H-series mobile processors, marking a near-half-decade processor architecture jump over the Raptor Lake-based MS-01.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: Confirms Panther Lake design wins in the homelab/SFF segment, a leading indicator of Intel's mobile SoC volume adoption beyond laptops.
insights.quote: the MS-03 is based around Intel’s Core Ultra Series 3 (Panther Lake) H-series mobile processors
insights.locator: 'At its heart, the MS-03 is based around...'

insights.claim: Minisforum deliberately chose regular Panther Lake H-series chips over the more powerful X7/X9 iGPU SKUs because the X-series parts offer fewer PCIe lanes, prioritizing expandability over graphics.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: Shows PCIe lane count, not GPU power, is the binding design constraint for expandable mini-workstations, informing which Intel SKUs win in the homelab tier.
insights.quote: Minisforum opted against using the X7/X9 SKUs – which feature Intel’s most powerful iGPU configuration – as those parts offer fewer PCIe lanes than the regular H-series chips

insights.claim: The MS-03 is the first time Minisforum can offer an NPU in this SFF form factor, alongside DDR5-7200 support, Wi-Fi 7 plus Bluetooth 6 via the new BE211 adapter.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: On-device NPU reaching commodity mini-PCs broadens local AI inference hardware penetration into the homelab/edge segment.
insights.quote: this also marks the first time that Minisforum has been able to offer an NPU in this specific form factor
insights.locator: 'Besides the new SoC’s far more powerful CPU and GPU cores...'

insights.claim: On the MS-03 all three M.2 slots get at least Gen4 speeds (two at PCIe Gen5 x4), but the x16 slot was cut from Gen4 x8 to Gen5 x4 electrical, keeping PCIe-slot bandwidth flat generationally while freeing lanes for NVMe storage.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: Illustrates the zero-sum PCIe lane budgeting in small systems; storage bandwidth gains came at the expense of add-in card slot width.
insights.quote: all three slots get at least Gen4 speeds, with the two faster slots getting PCIe Gen5 x4 connections
insights.locator: 'The company has also reworked how they are allocating PCIe lanes...'

insights.claim: The MS-03 raises steady-state TDP to 70 Watts from the MS-01's 60 Watts while reusing the identical chassis unchanged since 2024, raising open questions about thermals and noise.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: A higher TDP in an unchanged cooling envelope is a thermal-risk flag worth watching in reviews before homelab buyers commit.
insights.quote: While the MS-01 ran with a steady-state TDP of 60 Watts, the MS-03 will be set to 70 Watts.
insights.locator: Finally, Minisforum is tuning the TDP

insights.claim: The author criticizes the MS-03 for retaining 2.5GbE RJ45 jacks instead of 10GBASE-T, which has fallen sharply in price over the last year, and for limited high-speed USB on the front in 2026.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: Signals that 10GBASE-T pricing has dropped enough to be a baseline expectation; vendors still shipping 2.5GbE risk looking dated, a competitive differentiator in homelab networking gear.
insights.quote: the use of 2.5GbE for the RJ45 jacks is a surprise when proper 10GBASE-T 10GbE has come down so much in the last year
